briefing.incidentSummary:
A photograph submitted as key evidence in a burglary case shows suspect Arjun Mehta near a research facility at 10:11 PM.

The suspect denies being there. Forensic analysts suspect the image may have been digitally manipulated.

Your job: prove whether this photo is real — or a frame-up.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Analyze the image for signs of manipulation

› Examine EXIF metadata for editing traces

› Cross-reference camera records with the photo timestamp

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Unknown Editor
suspects.profileNotes: Evidence strongly points to a skilled editor who obtained the original photograph, composited Arjun Mehta's image into the scene, and re-exported the file. The use of professional editing software is confirmed by EXIF metadata. True identity unestablished.
suspects.description: Unknown Editor is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Witness Photographer
suspects.profileNotes: Camera records do not match the evidence photo's timestamp. May have unknowingly shared an original image that was later manipulated by a third party. No confirmed motive to fabricate evidence. Camera sequence log is missing the 22:11 entry.
suspects.description: Witness Photographer is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Arjun Mehta
suspects.profileNotes: The suspect in the photograph. Denied being at the facility. No editing software found on his devices. No image access history. Evidence supports his claim that the photo is fabricated — but his motive for the burglary (wrongful dismissal dispute) remains noted.
suspects.description: Arjun Mehta is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Internal Staff Member
suspects.profileNotes: Had access to internal file systems. Could theoretically have distributed the image internally. However, no editing software access was found, and no motive has been established. Remains a peripheral suspect.
suspects.description: Internal Staff Member is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

items.label: crime_scene_photo.jpg — 5472 × 3648
items.detail: The photograph shows a dimly lit parking area near the facility's rear service entrance. A man matching Arjun Mehta's description is visible standing near the doorway. At first glance, lighting and shadows appear consistent with the environment. No obvious visual anomalies are visible without enhancement tools.

items.label: Edge Artifact Detected — Subject Boundary
items.detail: Zoom and contrast enhancement reveals faint blur artifacts along the edges of the subject's outline — particularly around the shoulders and hairline. These artifacts are consistent with image compositing: the subject was likely cut from a separate photograph and placed onto this background. The blur pattern is inconsistent with the camera's natural depth-of-field behavior at this distance.

minigame.title: Edge Artifact Inspector
minigame.hint: Use zoom and contrast sliders to reveal blur artifacts around the suspect in the image. Highlight the compositing boundary.
minigame.instruction: Drag the contrast slider up and zoom into the subject's outline. Flag the region where blur artifacts indicate image compositing.
hints: Look at the edges of the subject's body — not the background. Compositing artifacts appear where two images meet.
Increase contrast to 80%. The blur becomes visible around the shoulder and hairline boundary.
The correct region is the shoulder and hairline edge — this is where the subject was cut from a different photo and placed onto this background.

onSuccess.message: ANOMALY CONFIRMED — Edge artifacts detected. This image shows signs of compositing. The subject may have been inserted from a different photograph. EXIF Metadata panel now unlocked.

items.label: Camera Model — Canon R5
items.detail: Camera model listed as Canon R5. Consistent with the witness photographer's registered device. File format is JPEG. Resolution matches Canon R5 native output (5472 × 3648). At surface level, the metadata appears genuine.

items.label: Software Tag — Adobe Photoshop Detected
items.detail: Hidden within the EXIF data is a Software tag that reads 'Adobe Photoshop 2024 (Windows)'. This tag is automatically written by Photoshop whenever a file is saved or exported. It confirms that this JPEG was opened, edited, and re-exported using professional image editing software after it was originally captured. A photograph exported directly from a Canon R5 would carry the camera's own software tag — not Photoshop's.

items.label: camera_sequence_log.txt — Canon R5
items.detail: The camera's internal sequence log records every photo taken in chronological order by file number and timestamp. The log shows: IMG_1051 at 22:05, IMG_1052 at 22:06, IMG_1053 at 22:07 — then jumps directly to IMG_1055 at 23:14. There is no entry for IMG_1054, and no record of any photo taken at 22:11. The evidence photo is labeled IMG_1054 with a timestamp of 22:11 — a timestamp that does not appear anywhere in the camera's own internal log. This is a direct contradiction: the photo claims to come from this camera at 22:11, but the camera has no record of taking that shot.

timeline.title: Photographer Takes Photos
timeline.description: Witness captures IMG_1051–1053 near the facility. Camera log confirms all three entries.

timeline.title: Claimed Photo Timestamp
timeline.description: Evidence photo IMG_1054 is timestamped 22:11 — but this entry is MISSING from the camera log.

timeline.title: Image Edited in Photoshop
timeline.description: EXIF Software tag confirms the file was opened and re-exported using Adobe Photoshop 2024.

timeline.title: Photo Submitted as Evidence
timeline.description: Witness hands over the photograph to investigators as proof of Arjun Mehta's presence at the scene.

timeline.title: Forensic Analysis Requested
timeline.description: Suspect denies being at the scene. Forensic team begins authenticating the image.

updates.note: Multiple suspects appear involved after edge artifact discovery.
Photoshop tag shifts suspicion heavily toward an unknown editor. Arjun has no editing access.
Camera mismatch is damning. Photographer's credibility falls. Unknown editor rises to Critical.

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Image confirmed as fabricated. True editor identified as an unknown insider.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: You identified the right suspect but the evidence chain is incomplete.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: Arjun Mehta was framed. The real culprit was an unknown editor who manipulated the photograph.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

coldCase.title: Case Gone Cold
coldCase.subtitle: No evidence collected. The manipulated photo remains accepted as truth.
coldCase.label: COLD CASE

finalTwist.title: The Frame-Up Revealed
finalTwist.description: The photograph was originally taken by the witness — but was later obtained by an unknown third party, edited using professional software to composite Arjun Mehta into the scene, and re-submitted as authentic evidence. The scene itself may be real. The suspect placement is not.
finalTwist.conclusion:
✔ Image confirmed as manipulated
✔ Evidence authenticity compromised
✔ Arjun Mehta was framed
✔ True editor's identity remains unestablished
